title: Build customer and contact management by extending the installed CRM
prompt: In a fresh standalone Open Mercato app, deliver customer and contact management using the installed customers module as the customer identity and CRUD capability. Add app-owned records only for genuinely distinct concepts and use UMES for requested fields, policies, widgets, or related data. Include secure PII handling, scoped UI, translations, search, optimistic locking, migrations where app-owned data is needed, and integration coverage; do not clone the installed customer model.

title: Customize the installed CRM deals pipeline with tenant policies
prompt: Modify the installed customers module's deals pipeline with tenant-configurable stages, probabilities, required fields, transition validation, board and table rendering, ACL-aware actions, translations, and stale-write conflict handling. Preserve the shipped deal and pipeline identifiers and use UMES plus an app-owned policy record only when persistence is necessary; never edit installed package source.

title: Generate a public lead-capture app with idempotent CRM handoff
prompt: Generate a standalone app that gathers website leads and hands qualified submissions into the installed customers CRM, including its deal capabilities. Include a localized public form, server-derived scope, spam and consent validation, encrypted PII, idempotent submission keys, a lead inbox, deterministic deduplication, qualification workflow, customer and deal matching or creation, attribution, and retries without duplicate CRM records. Keep CRM coupling optional and use IDs, events, commands, and UMES rather than cross-module ORM relationships.

title: Add customer success health and renewal management to CRM
prompt: Build a customer-success app module that adds health scores, account ownership, renewal dates, risk reasons, tasks, and reminder automation to installed CRM customers. Provide enriched customer lists and details, an editable panel, renewal filters, notifications, durable escalation, scoped APIs, and safe degradation when optional CRM or workflow capabilities are unavailable.

title: Build a scoped sales forecast and pipeline coaching dashboard
prompt: Build a sales forecasting and pipeline-coaching app module over the installed customers module's deals. Include stage-weighted forecasts, owner and period filters, currency-aware totals, historical snapshots, drill-down tables, stale-deal indicators, export, translations, ACL-filtered aggregates, and tenant-scoped caching.

title: Add customer-specific assortments and exact catalog pricing
prompt: Build customer-specific assortments and price lists using app-owned assignment and pricing records linked by scalar IDs to installed customers and catalog products. Include effective dates, currency-aware decimal prices, deterministic rule precedence, catalog enrichment, assignment UI, guarded mutations, cache invalidation, optimistic locking, and safe behavior when an optional host module is disabled.

title: Add quote approval and exactly-once conversion into orders
prompt: Build configurable quote-approval policies over installed sales quotes. Include submission, manager approval and rejection, durable user tasks, separation-of-duties ACL, guarded conversion of approved quotes into orders, aggregate optimistic locking, audit events, notifications, retries, and proof that conversion happens exactly once.

title: Build returns and RMA management across orders and portal
prompt: Build an RMA app module linked by scalar IDs and snapshots to installed orders, products, customers, and the customer portal. Support return requests, item eligibility, approval, receiving, disposition, guarded refunds, portal status, admin actions, notifications, and durable transitions. Prevent returned quantities from exceeding fulfillment and keep retries and refund actions idempotent.

title: Build payment reconciliation and dispute management
prompt: Build payment reconciliation and dispute handling using installed payment-gateway, data-sync, progress, sales, and workflow capabilities. Import or synchronize provider statements, deterministically match payments, retain unmatched exceptions, reconcile exact money totals, manage locked dispute cases, show progress and reports, and make repeated or concurrent runs produce no duplicate effects.

title: Build provider-neutral carrier selection and fulfillment tracking
prompt: Build a provider-neutral fulfillment app module that compares installed carrier quotes, selects the cheapest valid service using deterministic tenant rules, books shipments, obtains labels, and tracks delivery. Include order widgets, progress, exceptions, retry-safe booking, address and parcel validation, webhook or polling convergence, and safe failure when a provider is absent.

title: Let scoped customer users view orders and approve quotes in the portal
prompt: Extend the customer portal so authenticated customer users can list and view their quotes and orders, approve eligible quotes, receive notifications, and navigate from the portal sidebar. Derive customer and organization scope from the portal principal, enforce backend ACL independently from UI visibility, guard the sales aggregate with optimistic locking, and cover mobile, loading, empty, denial, conflict, and error states.

title: Synchronize CRM customers and catalog products with an ERP
prompt: Build a reusable ERP DataSync provider for customers and products. Include encrypted credentials and health checks, inbound and outbound mappings, stable external IDs, scoped cursors, deterministic conflict ownership, resumable queue execution, progress UI, connection tests, manual retry and reconciliation, and proof that a failed page never advances its cursor or duplicates committed records.

title: Write focused unit tests for command invariants and failure paths
prompt: For an app-owned library checkout command, write focused unit tests before changing behavior. Cover successful checkout, authorization denial, tenant and organization isolation, malformed input, unavailable copy, stale aggregate version, injected atomic-write failure, and idempotent retry. Use the repository's existing test runner and fixtures, then run the smallest focused unit-test command and report the exact result.

title: Build self-contained API integration tests for a CRUD slice
prompt: Create and run self-contained integration tests for a new app-owned library books CRUD API. Create all tenant, organization, user, role, and book fixtures through supported setup or API helpers; cover list, detail, create, update, delete, denial, cross-organization isolation, invalid filters, and stale If-Match conflicts; clean every created record in finally; never rely on demo or seeded data. Use the external om-integration-tests workflow and report the filtered command and result.

title: Build and run self-contained browser integration coverage
prompt: Create and run a self-contained browser integration test for a customer-portal quote approval flow. Prepare an ephemeral environment, create all actors and records through API fixtures, exercise navigation, loading, empty, validation, denial, mobile, successful approval, and stale-conflict states with semantic locators, verify the backend result, and clean up in finally. Use om-integration-tests and do not depend on demo data, arbitrary sleeps, or a developer's existing browser session.

title: Run affected tests first and escalate to the configured release gate
prompt: Validate a completed standalone module change that touched entities, commands, CRUD routes, backend pages, translations, events, and generated discovery. Run generation first, then the smallest affected unit and filtered integration tests, followed by the required typecheck, lint, test, build, and ephemeral-integration tail for this broad contract change. Re-run dependent gates after generated changes and report every command, runner, exit result, and any blocker honestly.

title: Map a complex module brief onto the complete discovery surface catalog
prompt: For a standalone service-management module that needs entities, validators, commands, APIs, backend and public pages, page middleware, UI locales, ACL/setup/DI/encryption, search, vector config, analytics, typed events, async and sync query subscribers, workers, workflows, progress, notifications, reactive handlers, messages, widgets, command/API interceptors, response/query enrichers, guards, AI tools, CLI, translatable fields, and tests, map each named capability to the current canonical discovery or support surface. Distinguish generator discovery from imported support files; reject retired flat pages, HTTP-method API directories, data/fields.ts, and unnecessary placeholders; include generation and focused verification.

title: Select every supported additive UMES extension mechanism correctly
prompt: Audit how a standalone app can add behavior to installed modules without replacing them. Cover response and query-engine enrichers; API/command interceptors and mutation guards; CrudForm and DataTable/menu/rendered widget injection including client operation filters; entity extensions; async, sync, and query-lifecycle subscribers; reactive notification handlers and DOM Event Bridge hooks; integration wizard/status/detail widgets; vector config; embedded AiChat; typed payment/shipping/currency/workflow registrations; and component props/wrappers. For each, name the smallest mechanism and specialist route, exact host-token evidence, authorization boundary, optional-host behavior, read/write/UI round trip where applicable, and verification; do not use unified replacement when additive composition works.

title: Audit all wired unified module override domains and stable keys
prompt: Audit the canonical src/modules.ts entry.overrides surface for a standalone app. Account for all wired domains: AI agents/tools plus additive AI extensions; API and backend/frontend page routes; subscribers; workers; injection, component, and dashboard widgets; notification types/handlers; API and command interceptors; enrichers; page guards; CLI; entry-scoped setup; ACL; DI; and encryption maps. State exact resolved keys (including registry entry keys and worker metadata fallback), which maps support null-disable/typed replacement, the special AI-extension and setup shapes, where global override maps may live, bootstrap/generation implications, stale-key diagnostics, security preservation, and rollback verification.

title: Build a responsive public frontend over installed catalog capabilities
prompt: Build an app-owned public product-discovery frontend over the installed catalog without editing framework packages. Use canonical frontend page and page metadata discovery, explicit public/auth posture, server-first data and a narrow deterministic client boundary, localized search/filter/detail/empty/error states, semantic design-system components and tokens, mobile-first responsive layout, accessible keyboard/focus behavior, and self-contained browser integration coverage. Reuse installed APIs or an app-owned scoped facade rather than importing private module files.

title: Extend the customer portal with scoped navigation and complete UX states
prompt: Add an app-owned customer support cases page to the installed portal. Use frontend/[orgSlug]/portal/**/page.tsx with sibling metadata, customer auth/features for guarded pages while preserving public-page semantics, principal-derived scope, optional nav only for the list destination, frozen portal hooks/spots/handles, portal menu injection only for links without a backing page, DataTable for the list, shared responsive and accessible components, loading/empty/not-found/denial/error/conflict/success states, wildcard-aware authorization, direct-route protection, and portal integration tests.

title: Redesign an operations page with complete design-system and UX compliance
prompt: Review and redesign a dense standalone operations page that currently uses raw forms, hand-built cards, hard-coded status colors, arbitrary Tailwind sizes, inline SVG, fixed desktop widths, vague errors, window.confirm, and inaccessible icon actions. Preserve behavior while selecting shared page/table/form/detail/chart/banner components, the current Alert and confirmation APIs, standalone FormField versus CrudForm-owned layout, semantic tokens, system-status versus user-tag semantics, approved brand-gradient exceptions, mobile-first standard breakpoints and long translations, loading/empty/not-found/denial/validation/network/conflict/success states, keyboard/dialog/focus/screen-reader behavior, reduced motion, and real API/UI verification.
